assertions.expectation: Explains that surge upgrades require extra compute capacity (maxSurge creates NEW nodes before draining old ones)
assertions.reason: Response explains that surge nodes can't be created due to quota constraints and describes drain-first strategy as creating no extra nodes

assertions.expectation: Recommends setting maxUnavailable >0 instead of relying on maxSurge (drains first, then creates — uses less quota)
assertions.reason: Explicitly recommends Option 1 with maxUnavailable=1 and maxSurge=0 as the drain-first strategy

assertions.expectation: Warns that maxUnavailable >0 means reduced capacity during upgrade — some pods won't have nodes
assertions.reason: States 'Temporary capacity loss (one node at a time is unavailable)' and 'capacity dip is brief' as trade-offs

assertions.expectation: Suggests temporarily scaling down non-critical deployments to free up quota for surge nodes
assertions.reason: Option 3 provides specific kubectl scale commands to scale down dev/test and canary deployments

assertions.expectation: Mentions that reducing maxSurge to 1 minimizes extra quota needed (slower but fits within constraints)
assertions.reason: Option 2 shows maxSurge=1 and explains 'creates only 1 surge node at a time — much slower but fits minimal extra quota'

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command to update surge settings (maxSurge=0, maxUnavailable=1 for zero-extra-capacity mode)
assertions.reason: Provides exact gcloud command with --max-surge-upgrade 0 and --max-unavailable-upgrade 1

assertions.expectation: Recommends doing the upgrade in off-peak hours when fewer pods are running
assertions.reason: Option 4 specifically mentions scheduling during off-peak hours (nights/weekends) when autoscaler has scaled down

assertions.expectation: Mentions requesting a temporary quota increase as a faster alternative to a permanent increase
assertions.reason: Does not suggest requesting a temporary quota increase - only provides workarounds for the existing quota constraint
